title: Alon–Krivelevich–Sudakov Max-Cut Exponent Conjecture
statement_text: For $H$ fixed and $m$ sufficiently large, every $H$-free graph $G$ with $m$ edges satisfies $\mathrm{Max\text{-}Cut}(G) \geq \frac{m}{2} + \Omega_H\!\left(m^{3/4}\right)$.
context_text: Alon, Krivelevich, and Sudakov proved that for fixed $H$ and large $m$, every $H$-free graph $G$ with $m$ edges satisfies $\mathrm{Max\text{-}Cut}(G) \geq \frac{m}{2} + m^{1/2+\varepsilon(H)}$ for some $\varepsilon(H)>0$, and conjectured the exponent $1/2$ can be replaced by $3/4$. The paper studies this in the special case $H = C_k$ for odd $k$.
paper_contribution: Theorem 1.7 proves $\mathrm{Max\text{-}Cut}(G) \geq \frac{m}{2} + \Omega_k(m^{(k+5)/(k+7)})$ for $C_k$-free graphs with odd $k \geq 3$; since $(k+5)/(k+7) > 3/4$ for all odd $k \geq 3$, this confirms the conjecture for $H = C_k$ with odd $k \geq 3$.

title: Alon et al. $C_k$-Free Max-Cut Conjecture
statement_text: For all $k \geq 3$, every $C_k$-free graph $G$ with $m$ edges satisfies $\mathrm{Max\text{-}Cut}(G) \geq \frac{m}{2} + \Omega_k\!\left(m^{(k+1)/(k+2)}\right)$.
context_text: Alon et al. conjectured this bound for all $k \geq 3$ and verified it for even $k$, showing the bound is tight for $k \in \{4, 6, 10\}$. For odd $k$, the best previously known lower bound was $\frac{m}{2} + m^{(k+1)/(k+3)+o(1)}$ due to Zeng and Hou.
paper_contribution: Theorem 1.7 proves $\mathrm{Max\text{-}Cut}(G) \geq \frac{m}{2} + \Omega_k(m^{(k+5)/(k+7)})$ for odd $k \geq 3$, improving the Zeng–Hou exponent $(k+1)/(k+3)+o(1)$ toward the conjectured exponent $(k+1)/(k+2)$.
notes: A footnote in the paper clarifies that reference [9] actually conjectured only that the bound is tight for even $k$; the full conjecture for all $k \geq 3$ as stated may be attributed differently in the literature.

title: Conjecture on sharpness of Theorem 1.1
statement_text: For each integer $r \geq 3$ there is $c_r$ such that for all positive integers $n, k$ with $n$ sufficiently large in terms of $k$, $$h(n, k, K_r) = \Theta_r\!\left(\frac{n^2}{k^{(r-1)/(r-2)}}\right),$$ i.e., the upper bound $h(n, k, K_r) \leq c_r \dfrac{n^2}{k^{(r-1)/(r-2)}}$ is tight up to the constant factor $c_r$.
context_text: Theorem 1.1 establishes the upper bound $h(n,k,K_r) \leq c_r n^2/k^{(r-1)/(r-2)}$ for all $r \geq 3$. The authors prove the matching lower bound for $r=3$ and conjecture the bound is sharp for all $r \geq 3$.

title: Conjecture on sharpness of Theorem 1.5
statement_text: For each positive integer $r$, the bound $$h(n, k, W_{2r+1}) \leq c_r \frac{n^2}{k^{2 - 1/(r+1)}}$$ is tight up to the constant factor $c_r$ depending on $r$.
context_text: Theorem 1.5 gives the upper bound $h(n,k,W_{2r+1}) \leq c_r n^2/k^{2-1/(r+1)}$ for odd wheels when $n \geq k \geq 2$. The authors conjecture this is tight, by analogy with their matching lower bounds in the odd-cycle case.

title: Question 2.1
statement_text: Given a graph $G$, how many edges of $G$ can we cover by the union of $k$ neighborhoods of vertices of $G$?
context_text: The authors define $u(G,k)$ as the maximum number of edges covered by the union of $k$ vertex neighborhoods and study $m(n,k) = \min_G(e(G) - u(G,k))$. Bounds on this quantity are the main tool for proving upper bounds on $h(n,k,H)$ when $H$ is a clique or an odd wheel.

title: Open problem on $m(n,k)$ for fixed $k \geq 2$
statement_text: What is the exact or asymptotic value of $m(n,k)$ for any fixed $k \geq 2$?
context_text: The authors prove $m(n,k) \leq n^2/(ek)$ (Corollary 2.6) and derive a lower bound from an Erdős–Rényi random graph heuristic that is within 20% of this upper bound for large $k$. They state explicitly: 'We have no idea what the exact or asymptotic value of $m(n,k)$ is for any fixed $k \geq 2$.'
notes: Stated as an informal open problem in the body of the paper without a labelled theorem environment.
